mnemonics: M - Master the Basics of NumPy Arrays
N - Numbers in NumPy are N-dimensional
A - Arrays are the foundation of NumPy
P - Pythagorean Theorem for Array operations
S - Structured arrays for complex data
S - Sorting arrays for efficient data
S - Structured arrays for data with varying types
keyPoints: A NumPy array is a collection of values of the same data type
Arrays are 0-indexed, meaning the first element is at index 0
Sorting arrays is an efficient operation in NumPy
Structured arrays are useful for data with varying types
NumPy arrays can be resized using the `reshape()` function
NumPy arrays support broadcasting for operations with arrays of different shapes
